topic: Calculating budget shortfall and making cuts
title: Solving the Schofields' budget crisis
instruction: Use the information to calculate the Schofield family's new budget situation.
context: Original income: £2,600/month. After 15% cut: £2,210/month. New expenses: Rent £880 | Energy £180 | Food £378 | Transport £120 | Phone/internet £80 | Childcare £400 | Clothing £60 | Entertainment £90 | Other £50 | New total expenses: £2,238.

questions.question: The family's expenses are £2,238 but income is £2,210. What is their monthly shortfall?
Which expense would you cut first to help the family? Choose the most sensible option.
options: Childcare — they can look after the children themselves
Entertainment — it is the least essential expense
Rent — they can negotiate with the landlord
questions.answer: Entertainment — it is the least essential expense

questions.question: After cutting entertainment (£90) and reducing clothing by 50% (saving £30), what is the new monthly surplus?
The family cuts entertainment fully (£90) and clothing by half (saves £30). What are new total expenses?
With new income £2,210 and new expenses £2,118, what is the monthly surplus?
If they save this surplus for 12 months, how much will they have saved?
